pi_items.source: task.json description specifies 'exactly three pages' and system_spec.txt lines 134-149 lists 3 required pages
verifier.py line 130 and README.md success criteria; actual spec requires 8 (1 system + 3 users + 4 external systems)
verifier.py line 146 and README.md success criteria; actual spec lists 12 microservices
system_spec.txt line 31; confirmed as real Microsoft reference application at https://github.com/dotnet-architecture/eShopOnContainers
eShopOnContainers is a real, official Microsoft reference application for cloud-native microservices per Microsoft Learn and GitHub
system_spec.txt lines 57-120 lists: Web MVC App, SPA Client, Mobile App, API Gateway, Identity.API, Catalog.API, Basket.API, Ordering.API, Payment.API, Marketing.API, Location.API, Event Bus

system_spec.txt claims Stripe Payment Gateway and SendGrid Email Service, but these are not documented in official eShopOnContainers sources (Microsoft Learn, GitHub README). These appear to be task-specific additions, not part of actual eShopOnContainers.
Azure Service Bus is confirmed in eShopOnContainers via Microsoft Learn documentation and GitHub wiki for production message broker
Application Insights mentioned as telemetry/monitoring option in system_spec.txt; supported by Azure integration in eShopOnContainers
C4 model is a real methodology created by Simon Brown; confirmed via c4model.com and multiple sources
Microsoft has published '.NET Microservices: Architecture for Containerized .NET Applications' available at learn.microsoft.com, updated 2022
Verified as real GitHub repository; noted as archived/moved to https://github.com/dotnet/eShop as of November 2023

privileged_info_summary: eShopOnContainers is a real, official Microsoft reference application for cloud-native microservices published at https://github.com/dotnet-architecture/eShopOnContainers (now moved to https://github.com/dotnet/eShop). It demonstrates microservices architecture with 12 internal containers/services (Catalog.API, Basket.API, Ordering.API, Identity.API, Payment.API, Marketing.API, Location.API, API Gateway, and others) communicating via an event bus using RabbitMQ (development) or Azure Service Bus (production). The C4 model for architecture documentation is a real methodology by Simon Brown. The task requires creating a 3-page C4 diagram (System Context, Container Diagram, and Legend) with minimum shape counts of 6 and 10 respectively, saving to /home/ga/Diagrams/ecommerce_c4_model.drawio and exporting to PDF. However, the specification includes Stripe and SendGrid as external systems, which are not documented in official eShopOnContainers sources and appear to be task-specific additions. The output file paths, page structure, and system name are all verified against task description and system specification.
